
Energy Balance
$$\begin{split} &   
\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \sum w_i+ \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \sum \mathbf{\mathscr{S}}_i + \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} = 0 
\end{split}$${#eq-energbal} 

Abbreviations:

$w_i$ = engery densities (electric, magnetic, gravitational, etc. ...)

$\mathbf{\mathscr{S}}_i$ = energy transport densities (transfer) by fields (electric, magnetic, gravitational, etc. ...) 

$\boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E}$ = energy gain provided by fields $\mathscr{E}$ and $\mathscr{B}$ through current density $j$



### Mechanical
...

### Electromagnectic energy transfer

{{< include C:/Users/erine/Desktop/Physics-Explorations/GitHub-Physics/PhysEcon/Content/Continuity.qmd >}}

$\boldsymbol{j} = \sigma \left( \mathscr{E} + v \times \mathscr{B} \right) = \nabla \times \mathscr{H} - \frac{\partial}{\partial t}\mathscr{D} \hat{=}$ elecectric current density

$\boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} \hat{=}$ energy gain by fields $\mathscr{E}$ and $\mathscr{B}$ through current density $j$

$\mathscr{S} = \mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H} \hat{=}$ energy transport density (Intensity = $\left| \bar{\mathscr{S}} \right|$)


Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium:

$$\begin{split} &   
\int    \left(
\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \left( w_e + w_m \right) + \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} + \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} 
\right) \, dr
= \frac{d}{dt}W_e + \frac{d}{dt}W_m + D_S + \int \left( \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} \right) \, dr 
\newline & 
\stackrel{\textnormal{low frequency}}{=}
\frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( LI^2 \right) + \frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( \frac{Q^2}{C} \right) + \left( RI^2 - VI \right) = 0
\end{split}$${#eq-energyconserv-1} 

Abreviations:
$$
\Delta \cdot \coloneqq \textnormal{ div grad = Laplacian}
$$\linebreak$$  
J = \textnormal{"ignition of inhomogenity,cause"} \quad
j = \textnormal{Current density} \quad
\varrho = \textnormal{Charge density}
$$\linebreak$$  
V = \textnormal{Potentialdifference} \quad  
I = \textnormal{Current} \quad
R = \textnormal{Resistance} \quad
$$\linebreak$$  
L = \textnormal{Inductance} \qquad  
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
Q = c\textnormal{harge (ion)} \qquad
\Psi = LI = \textnormal{mag. Flux}
$$\linebreak$$ 
\mathscr{S} = \mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H} = \textnormal{Poynting-Vector, radiation intensity}
$$\linebreak$$
D_S = \int \left( \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} \right) \, dr = \int \textnormal{div}(\mathscr{E} \times \mathscr{H}) \, dr = \textnormal{dissipation engergy lost from radiation} \approx \textnormal{0 for low frequencies } \omega
$$\linebreak$$
d = \sqrt{\frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{\omega}} = \textnormal{skin depth (surface) of conductor} \qquad
r = \frac{d}{\sqrt{2i}}\rho = \frac{d}{1+i}\rho = \textnormal{radius (extension) of conductor}
$$\linebreak$$
\rho = kr = \textnormal{"radius of curvature $k$"}
$$\linebreak$$ 
\mu = \textnormal{permeability of conductor}    \qquad
\sigma = \textnormal{conductivity of conductor (dielectricum)} \qquad
$$


### Thermal
...


### Ralativistc
...
